title: Parent phase map targets an obsolete 16-phase topology
claim: The active parent topology remains in conflict with the authoritative phase tree.
recommendation: Regenerate the parent phase map from the authoritative phase tree.
scopeProof: Repeated active finding; no target modification.

title: Phase 000 requires unavailable predecessor and rename-map evidence
claim: Phase 000 cannot satisfy its own blocking checklist because it is the first phase, while the checklist requires landed predecessors and a rename-map hash owned by a later phase.
recommendation: Make phase-000 evidence phase-local and defer rename-map hashing to phase 006 onward.
scopeProof: Repeated active finding; no target modification.

title: Duplicate partial phase map competes with the packet topology
claim: The second parent phase documentation map presents only phases 009–011 as the aggregate progress map, creating a competing and incomplete navigation contract.
counterevidence: The second map could be a narrow final-phase handoff view, but it calls itself the parent phase documentation map and aggregate progress source rather than a scoped appendix.
alternativeExplanation: The partial table was left by a template scaffold. That still creates a stale source of truth because its transition rules apply it to the whole packet.
recommendation: Remove the duplicate map or generate one unambiguous map from the canonical graph/manifest; keep any final-phase view explicitly scoped and non-authoritative.
scopeProof: The issue is limited to parent documentation maintainability; no production or target artifact was changed.

focus: Maintainability: duplicated parent phase maps and authoritative navigation

results.evidence: F003: a second phase map conflicts with graph metadata as the usable navigation source.
This pass assessed parent-map maintainability rather than a phase implementation checklist.

coverage.nextFocus: broaden to worktree lifecycle and plan-to-workflow alignment
